title: epipactis neglecta
intro: Epipactis neglecta, also known as the woodcock orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Epipactis neglecta include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
